sections.title: A: Gap Fill (Book Review)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Rarely (1) ...... one find as clear an account of social change as Olivia Harris's Changing Britain.
Those who prefer to bury their heads in the (2) ...... and imagine that Britain is the same as it was even 20 years ago are in for a shock.
Harris convincingly argues that (3) ...... only have those (4) ...... authority increased their power, but ordinary people are also less likely to stand (5) ...... to those in office.
At (6) ...... point in the book (7) ...... any of Harris's claims presented without evidence, and neither does she talk (8) ...... to the non-expert.
Her main conclusion, that (9) ...... until we all realise that there is a problem and decide to confront the powers that (10) ...... will the situation improve, is powerful and irresistible.

sections.title: B: Word Formation
sections.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals.

items.sentence: The local people then leave the clay to ...... in the sun, creating simple bricks. **HARD**
The attack in the city centre last night ...... the kind of behaviour amongst young people that many object to. **EXAMPLE**
I was a bit ...... by my performance in the first exam, but I decided to make an extra effort in the ones left. **MORAL**
When confronted with a mass of red tape, many people feel a sense of ...... . **POWER**
I really believe that it would be a major mistake to ...... any drugs that are currently illegal. **CRIME**
Local elections can often seem ...... , but in fact they send an important signal to the government. **SIGNIFY**
Suddenly, without ...... , the dog sank its teeth into my leg. **PROVOKE**
There's so much fighting between rival groups that the country has become practically ...... . **GOVERN**

sections.title: C: Collocations / Prepositions
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: I'm in bed by eleven when I've got lectures the next day ...... a rule.
Am I right ...... saying that you used to live in Saudi Arabia?
Of course, this decision is subject ...... confirmation by the whole company board.
I've ...... my sentence, but it's not easy fitting back into society.
The hotel manager promised to keep my valuables ...... lock and key.
Politicians should remember that no one is ...... the law.
The rebel group in the north of the country have laid ...... their arms and agreed to discuss the situation.
The headteacher decided to ...... an example of Richard and exclude him from the school.

sections.title: D: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.

items.sentence:
I'd like to remind you that there are no circumstances in which employees are allowed to leave early. **under**
I'd like to remind you that employees ...... allowed to leave early.

I wouldn't confront the manager if I were you. **horns**
I wouldn't ...... the manager if I were you.

Passengers can only board the plane when all bags have been checked. **after**
Only ...... board the plane.
items.answer: after all bags have been checked can passengers

items.sentence:
The website wouldn't allow me to access certain pages. **denied**
The website ...... certain pages.

The kids dived straight into the pool when we arrived. **had**
No ...... the kids dived into the pool.

A reliable source told me that the local newspaper is going to shut down. **authority**
I ...... that the local newspaper is going to shut down.

There was so much concern about the situation that the police were called. **so**
The police were called, ...... there.

Why do you think you are allowed to criticise me like that? **gives**
What do you think ...... to criticise me like that?
There has been a lot of ...... surrounding the government's proposed scheme.
Our town has a real problem with youth crime, ...... do many other British towns.
Warning: anyone caught stealing from these premises will be ...... .
The local authorities need to ...... down on illegal parking, in my opinion.
If the service isn't up to standard, I think you have ...... right to complain.
Jim's a tough character and certainly won't let anyone push him ...... .
The ...... I don't understand is why Emily lets her boyfriend get away with it.
My uncle pulled a few ...... and got me a job in the company where he works.
